timeline.label: Q1 2026: revenues $2.06B (+15% YoY); diluted EPS $1.55 (+72% YoY); operating profit $644M
FY2025: revenues $8.21B (+8.8%); system sales $68.3B (+4%); same-store sales +3%; OCF $2.01B; repurchased $552M; dividend $2.84/sh
Scott Mezvinsky named CEO, KFC Division (from Pizza Hut CEO role)
Acquired KFC U.K. and Ireland stores from franchisee (store acquisition)
Accelerated digital/AI platform (Byte by Yum!) and global franchise development post-pandemic normalization
Suspended KFC/Pizza Hut operations in Russia; re-franchising and brand exit costs
Acquired Habit Burger & Grill (~300 units) for ~$375M to enter fast-casual burger segment
Spun off Yum China (YUMC) as independent listed company; YUM retained ex-China KFC, Taco Bell, Pizza Hut
Completed separation from Yum! Restaurants International structure; pure-play franchisor model accelerated
Spun off from PepsiCo; became independent public company Yum! Brands

industry.structure: Global quick-service restaurant oligopoly at the brand level: Yum! operates four concepts (KFC, Taco Bell, Pizza Hut, Habit) across 155 countries with 63,285 units, 97% franchised. Revenue is primarily franchise royalties and fees on $68.3B system sales, not store-level revenue.
industry.share_shift: Taco Bell gaining U.S. traffic and average check; KFC growing internationally (99% franchised, 90% ex-U.S. units); Pizza Hut losing share to delivery-native pizza competitors in key markets.
industry.trend: Franchisors investing in digital ordering, AI-assisted operations, and asset-light unit growth; consumer value sensitivity and labor inflation pressure franchisee margins while brand-level fee streams remain resilient.
